extension.title: Extension
extension.description: Install the LKS TV extension for a better experience
extension.step4: Drag the file into the page
extension.heroDesc: Unlock all video sources by extracting streams directly from your browser.
extension.whyDesc: Some video hosts require that stream extraction and playback happen on the same device and from their own domain. The extension solves this problem.
extension.diagramRequestServer: LKS TV requests the stream from the server
extension.diagramLocalExtract: The extension extracts the stream locally
extension.diagramLegitRequest: The host sees a legitimate request
extension.diagramSameDevice: Extraction and playback on the same device ✓
extension.benefitLocalDesc: Video streams are extracted directly on your device, without going through a third-party server. Faster and more reliable.
extension.benefitBypassDesc: Some hosts verify that the stream is extracted and played from the same device and domain. The extension acts from your browser.
extension.benefitSameDeviceDesc: These hosts require extraction and playback on the same machine. The extension natively respects this constraint.
extension.benefitCORSDesc: The extension injects the necessary headers and bypasses CORS protections that prevent stream playback from a third-party site.
extension.hostersDesc: The extension supports video stream extraction from these hosts.
extension.installDesc: Choose your installation method, then follow the guide step by step.
extension.installMethodFirefoxDesc: Recommended native extension for Firefox and Firefox-compatible browsers.
extension.installMethodUserscriptDesc: Tampermonkey-based alternative for Chrome, Edge, and Brave.
extension.installStep1Desc: Click the button below to open the installation method that matches your browser.
extension.installStep2Desc: Use the Firefox extension or the Tampermonkey userscript depending on your browser.
extension.installStep3Desc: Accept the requested permissions. The extension or userscript needs access to the network requests required to extract video streams.
extension.installStep4Desc: The extension is now active. Return to LKS TV and access all video sources without restriction.
extension.installFirefoxStep1Desc: Click the button below to open the extension page on Firefox Add-ons.
extension.installFirefoxStep2Desc: On the extension page, click "Add to Firefox" then confirm the installation.
extension.installFirefoxStep3Desc: Accept the requested permissions. The extension needs access to network requests to extract video streams.
extension.installFirefoxStep4Desc: The extension is now active. Return to LKS TV and access all video sources without restriction.
extension.installUserscriptStep1Desc: Add Tampermonkey to your browser so the LKS TV script can be loaded.
extension.installUserscriptStep2Desc: Open the install file, then confirm adding the userscript inside Tampermonkey.
extension.installUserscriptStep3Desc: Accept the requested permissions. The script needs to intercept the network requests required for video streams.
extension.installUserscriptStep4Desc: Reload LKS TV. The userscript mode is now active and unlocks compatible sources.
extension.compatibleBrowsers: Compatible with Firefox and Firefox-compatible browsers
extension.compatibleBrowsersUserscript: Compatible with Firefox via extension and Chrome, Edge, Brave via Tampermonkey userscript
extension.faq1Q: Why do I need this extension?
extension.faq1A: Some video hosts verify that the stream is extracted and consumed from the same device and browser. Without the extension, these streams cannot be played directly on LKS TV because the remote server detects that the request does not come from the right context.
extension.faq2A: Yes. The extension works exclusively on LKS TV domains, does not collect any personal data, and its code is open source on GitHub. It does not modify any web page outside of LKS TV.
extension.faq3Q: Does the extension work on mobile?
extension.faq3A: Chrome extensions are not available on mobile. On Android, you can use browsers like Kiwi Browser or Firefox that support extensions. On iOS, this is unfortunately not possible.
extension.faq4Q: What happens if I don't install the extension?
extension.faq4A: LKS TV will still work, but some video sources from protected hosts will not be available. Streams that require local extraction will be marked as unavailable.
extension.faq5Q: How do I know if the extension is active?
extension.faq5A: When the extension is correctly installed and active, an indicator appears on playback pages. Additionally, sources that require the extension become available in the player list.
extension.viewOpenSourceGithub: See our LKS TV open-source GitHub
extension.githubNotice: The extension and the userscript are available on the LKS TV open-source GitHub.
extension.ctaDesc: Install the LKS TV extension and access all available video sources without restriction.

top10.title: Top 10
top10.description: Discover the most watched movies, series and anime by the LKS TV community.
top10.noData: No data available yet for this category.
top10.fillsUp: The ranking fills up as the community watches content.
top10.howItWorksDesc: The LKS TV Top 10 is not an arbitrary ranking. It is calculated in real time from your actual viewing habits.
top10.moviesMinWatch: Minimum 20 minutes of watching to count
top10.seriesMinWatch: Minimum 5 minutes per episode to count
top10.loggedInUsersDesc: With a LKS TV account, your viewings count towards the ranking
top10.guestsAnonymousDesc: Sessions without an account do not count
top10.faq.q1: How does the Top 10 ranking work?
top10.faq.a1: The LKS TV Top 10 is built from actual viewing data of logged-in users. Every time you watch a movie or a series episode, your activity is recorded in aggregate. We don't make anything up: this ranking directly reflects what the community watches the most.
top10.faq.a2: We don't count accidental clicks or people who give up after 30 seconds. For a session to count as a view: you need to have watched at least 20 minutes for a movie, and at least 5 minutes for a series or anime. This ensures only real viewing sessions are included in the calculation.
top10.faq.a3: Several indicators are used to establish the ranking: the number of unique viewers (how many different people watched), total viewing time in hours, number of sessions, and average session duration. The main ranking is based on unique viewers, then on total time in case of a tie.
top10.faq.a4: No. Only logged-in users with a LKS TV account are included in the ranking. Anonymous browsing sessions or guest sessions are not recorded. This is what makes the data reliable: we know exactly who watches what.
top10.faq.q5: How often is the ranking updated?
top10.faq.a5: The data is recalculated regularly with a cache of a few minutes. The ranking evolves in near real-time as the community watches. If a movie suddenly buzzes, you'll see it climb quickly.
top10.faq.a6: First, because it's interesting to see what others are watching. But it's also a great way to discover popular content you might have missed. And conversely, it helps us identify what the LKS TV community really enjoys.
top10.loadError: Unable to load the ranking. Please try again later.
top10.evolvingRankingDesc: The more the community watches, the more precise the Top 10 becomes. Every viewing session counts. Come back regularly to see the trends.

dmca.title: DMCA
dmca.intro: Welcome to the DMCA page of LKS TV! We attach great value to the respect of intellectual property rights. If you consider that content available on our service infringes your copyright, we invite you to contact us at the email address mentioned below. We promise to process each legitimate request and promptly remove any content reported as infringing.
dmca.noStorage: We wish to clarify that LKS TV does not store any multimedia files on its servers. Our function consists solely of indexing publicly available content through external sources accessible online. All content listed on our site comes from third-party platforms over which we have no authority. Therefore, LKS TV assumes no responsibility regarding the availability, accuracy or legal compliance of files hosted by these external sources.
dmca.aggregator: We operate exclusively as a compilation and cataloging service, designed to simplify the search for content already present on the Internet. However, we fully honor copyright legislation and proactively cooperate with rights holders. When content is reported, we apply the required actions to remove it from our catalog.
dmca.dmcaNoticeIntro: To submit a compliant DMCA notice, please provide:
dmca.dmcaReq1: A detailed and accurate description of the copyrighted work.
dmca.dmcaReq2: The URLs or links directing to the disputed content on our site.
dmca.dmcaReq3: Your complete contact information (name, address, phone, email).
dmca.dmcaReq4: A statement confirming that your request is made in good faith and that the information provided is truthful.
dmca.thirdPartyWarning: We do not supervise or approve content accessible through third-party platforms. In case of dispute, we advise you to contact the hosts holding the original files directly for more details or to resolve disputes.
dmca.thanks: We express our gratitude for your understanding and cooperation. Your attention helps maintain LKS TV as a platform that respects copyright while offering a pleasant browsing experience to our users.

privacyPolicyPage.title: Privacy policy
privacyPolicyPage.eyebrow: Data processing actually present in LKS TV
privacyPolicyPage.seoDescription: LKS TV privacy policy: local storage, accounts, profiles, server sync, WatchParty, community features, security, third-party services and VIP.
intro: This page describes the data LKS TV actually processes as of March 29, 2026, based on the current behavior of the frontend, the main API, WatchParty, the bot, the browser extension, the userscript and VIP flows.
It covers what stays on your device, what can be synchronized server-side, what passes through third-party services and which controls are already visible in the interface.
summaryItems: LKS TV keeps a large part of your usage state locally in the browser.
Part of that state is synchronized server-side for connected Discord, Google or BIP39 accounts.
WatchParty, community features and VIP flows add their own logs, messages and technical data.
You can already disable future Wrapped collection and manage several items from the account settings.

localStorage.title: Data stored on your device
paragraphs: A significant part of the LKS TV experience runs directly in your browser. The site relies heavily on local storage to keep your preferences, session context and usage comfort between visits.
bullets: The browser may store settings, the selected profile ID, local session information, VIP states, watch history, favorites, custom lists, progress, episode alerts and other interface preferences.
The site's service worker caches some files to speed up loading, and the browser may keep additional temporary technical caches.
The browser extension and the userscript also keep local data such as activation state, runtime statistics and extraction caches.
The data exports and imports offered in settings remain under your control on your device until you choose to sync or share them.

accountsSync.title: Accounts, profiles and synchronization
bullets: When you sign in through Discord, Google or BIP39, LKS TV processes the matching account context: provider identifier, username, avatar, account type, sessions and active profile.
Session management notably stores a session identifier, the user agent, access dates and a derived encrypted device fingerprint built from the user agent and IP address.
Connected accounts can manage multiple profiles with name, avatar, age restriction and default profile; some community interactions are attached to a specific profile.
For connected Discord, Google and BIP39 accounts, part of the local state is synchronized server-side through deltas into persistent data tied to the account and profile.
Session secrets such as session_id, guest_uuid, auth_token and access_token are explicitly excluded from this automatic synchronization.
accountsSync.note: The current code disables server sync for guests. VIP users are also ignored by the automatic sync route.

usageHistory.title: Usage, history, Wrapped and personalization
bullets: LKS TV uses your preferences, playback progress, favorites, lists, alerts and settings to personalize the interface and restore your usage state.
When Wrapped collection is enabled, the frontend can send events including watch duration, visited page, content IDs and titles, season, episode, time and active profile.
An option in settings allows you to disable future Wrapped collection, and another allows you to limit or clear the local continue-watching history.
Episode alerts are stored locally and checked periodically in the browser to trigger client-side notifications.

communityWatchparty.title: Community, public content and WatchParty
bullets: Comments, replies, shared lists, votes, Wishboard, link submissions, reports, notifications and moderation elements may be stored server-side.
Comments and replies record the IP address along with account and profile identifiers so publishing, moderation and security can work.
Some community data is public by nature, for example shared lists in the public catalog or content visible to other users.
WatchParty processes room codes, nicknames, participants, messages, reactions, playback state, media metadata, room settings and sync votes.
Public WatchParty rooms expose some metadata publicly such as the code, title, poster, media type and participant count.
WatchParty rooms are kept in memory with temporary JSON persistence, limited to 100 messages per room, and automatically expire after 12 hours or after 3 hours of inactivity if empty.

securitySupport.title: Security, anti-abuse, VIP and support
bullets: LKS TV verifies Cloudflare Turnstile tokens for some sensitive actions, transmitting the token and remote IP address for anti-abuse purposes.
Some text content such as comments, nicknames or shared lists may be sent through automated moderation tools before or after publication, in addition to human review.
The VIP area processes invoice and payment data such as public invoice identifier, unique crypto address, expected amount, status, confirmations, transaction hash, gifts and VIP keys.
Hashed IP fingerprints are used in VIP flows for fraud prevention around invoice creation or gift opening.
In case of operational errors, some technical diagnostic elements may be sent to internal support tools, especially through Discord webhooks.
If you use Discord, Telegram or the official bot for support, your identifiers, messages, tickets, transcripts or payment references may be processed in that context.

thirdParties.title: Third-party services involved
bullets: Discord OAuth for identity and some server or member information needed by the site's Discord flows.
Google OAuth for email- and profile-based authentication.
TMDB for metadata, posters, logos and other catalog assets.
Cloudflare Turnstile for anti-bot verification.
Vercel Analytics for traffic and usage measurements on the site.
OpenRouter for some automated moderation of text content.
Coinbase, CoinGecko and BlockCypher for pricing and VIP payment verification.
External hosts, CDNs, proxies and media sources that receive the normal technical metadata of a request when playback or extraction is started.
thirdParties.note: Each third-party service applies its own rules. This page describes how LKS TV calls them today, not those providers' internal policies.

controlsContact.title: Your choices, retention and contact
paragraphs: LKS TV data may live in several places depending on the feature: in your browser, in JSON files tied to your account, in MySQL tables and in temporary technical caches. The code does not define one single uniform retention period for every case.
bullets: You can already manage some items directly in Settings: active sessions, linked accounts, local import or export, local history and future Wrapped collection.
Some deletions are local only and others require support intervention, because the repository does not expose a universal purge interface for all backend data.
LKS TV does not implement, in the current code, any mechanism to sell personal data to third parties.
Important changes to this page will be reflected by the update date shown here.

supportCard.title: Exercise control or ask a question
paragraphs: For a privacy question, an access request or a deletion request not covered by the available settings, use the contacts below.
For immediate settings already exposed in the app, the Settings page remains the main entry point.

privacyPolicyPage.footerNote: This policy reflects the observable behavior of the LKS TV code as of March 29, 2026. If a future change adds, removes or changes a significant data flow, this page should be aligned again with the real implementation.

termsOfServicePage.title: Terms of service
termsOfServicePage.seoDescription: LKS TV terms of service: accounts, WatchParty, community features, third-party services and VIP access.
intro: This page frames the practical use of LKS TV as reflected by the current state of the repository: browsing, playback, accounts, profiles, WatchParty, the extension, the community layer and VIP access.
It has been rewritten to match the real service, including third-party dependencies, technical limitations and moderation mechanisms already present in the code.
summaryItems: LKS TV partly relies on third-party services, sources and infrastructure whose availability may vary.
Accounts, profiles, WatchParty rooms, community content and VIP access may be limited or suspended in case of abuse or risk.
Some features require authentication, anti-bot verification, an extension or userscript, or a valid crypto invoice.
Continuing to use the service after this page changes means you accept the updated terms.

scope.title: Scope
paragraphs: These terms govern the use of LKS TV, including the main site, accounts and profiles, WatchParty, community features, VIP pages, the browser extension, the userscript and associated technical services.
By using LKS TV, you also accept the Privacy page and any specific rules displayed on certain features or forms.
bullets: LKS TV acts as an interface, aggregator, technical gateway and playback tool around sources, metadata and third-party services.
Some complementary pages, such as the DMCA page, set additional rules for specific cases.

accounts.title: Account, profiles and security
bullets: Access may use Discord, Google or BIP39. You remain responsible for the confidentiality of your BIP39 secret phrase, your linked accounts, your session tokens and the devices on which you sign in.
You are responsible for the actions performed through your sessions, selected profiles and WatchParty rooms until you have closed or revoked them.
You must not impersonate a third party, bypass authentication safeguards or use accounts, profiles or VIP access obtained fraudulently.
LKS TV may limit, close or reset sessions and account links when a security risk, abuse pattern or technical inconsistency is detected.
accounts.note: The BIP39 phrase acts as the primary access key to the affected account. Losing or disclosing it may prevent secure recovery or lead to unauthorized access.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable use
bullets: You must not use LKS TV to harass, spam, threaten, defraud, bypass applicable laws, publish clearly unlawful content or infringe the rights of others.
This notably forbids abusive automation, large-scale scraping, intentional infrastructure saturation, vote or report manipulation, and abuse of anti-bot, anti-abuse or session mechanisms.
You must not use WatchParty, comments, shared lists, suggestions, link submissions, proxies, the extension or the userscript in a way that disrupts the service or harms other users.
You remain responsible for complying with the terms of the third-party services you use through LKS TV.

thirdPartyServices.title: Third-party sources, playback and availability
bullets: LKS TV depends on third-party providers for metadata, images, authentication, some live channels, hosts, technical proxies, CDNs, verification services and payment services.
Some playback or extraction flows may require the browser extension, the userscript, local network permissions or additional technical components.
The availability of a stream, page, source or integration may change without notice depending on the state of external services, networks, browsers or devices.
LKS TV does not guarantee permanent availability, constant quality, universal compatibility or continuity of any given third-party source.
thirdPartyServices.note: External services and sources keep their own terms of service and privacy policies. Their behavior is not under LKS TV's exclusive control.

community.title: Community features and moderation
bullets: Comments, replies, shared lists, votes, Wishboard entries, link submissions, WatchParty nicknames, messages and reactions must remain lawful, respectful and technically manageable.
LKS TV may moderate some text content automatically and manually, refuse publication, hide an item, remove content or limit interactions when abuse, risk or a violation is detected.
Public lists, some votes, some community content and public WatchParty rooms may be visible to other users.
Repeated abuse may result in a feature suspension, session revocation, account restriction, loss of community access or removal of VIP benefits.

watchparty.title: WatchParty and real-time features
bullets: The creator of a WatchParty room remains responsible for invited people, room settings, the chosen sync mode and any content shared in the chat.
Public rooms may appear in the public catalog with visible metadata such as room code, title, poster, media type and participant count.
Real-time exchanges, playback state and room data are temporary and may be briefly persisted to ensure service continuity before automatic expiration.
Sync quality depends on devices, browser, network and external media sources. LKS TV does not guarantee perfect synchronization in every case.

vip.title: VIP, gifts and crypto donations
bullets: VIP access may rely on unique crypto invoices, gifts or access keys. Each invoice is time-limited and depends on a specific asset, network and amount.
Activation depends on on-chain detection, confirmations, anti-fraud checks and respect of the invoice validity window.
A payment sent on the wrong network, with the wrong asset, the wrong amount, after expiration or without enough confirmations may delay or prevent activation.
Invoices, gifts, VIP keys and related access may be suspended, refused or reviewed in case of abuse, technical inconsistency or suspected fraud.

changesLiability.title: Suspension, service changes and liability
bullets: LKS TV may modify, suspend or stop a feature, route, integration or access for technical, maintenance, security, compliance or operational reasons.
LKS TV may remove content, close a session, limit a room, revoke a linked account or restrict a user when risky behavior or a breach of these terms is observed.
The service is provided as is, with reasonable efforts toward stability, but without an absolute guarantee against errors, interruptions, third-party related loss or any specific outcome.
If one clause is held unenforceable, the others remain valid. Continuing to use the service after this page is updated means accepting the version in force.

supportCard.title: Useful links
paragraphs: The Privacy page complements these terms for the data and tracking aspects.
The DMCA page remains the right entry point for requests related to content and specific reports.

termsOfServicePage.footerNote: These terms describe the general rules governing the use of LKS TV as of March 29, 2026. Fraudulent, abusive or technically harmful behavior may trigger immediate restrictions even if a precise case is not listed word for word above.

dnsBlock.faiTooltip: Internet Service Provider — your carrier (Orange, SFR, Free, Bouygues, etc.). They're the ones blocking specific domains by court order (ARCOM).

helpDns.title: My ISP is blocking LKS TV
helpDns.heroSub: Install the Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 app to regain access to every source in 2 minutes.
helpDns.whyBody: French <1>ISPs</1> (Orange, SFR, Free, Bouygues) regularly block, by court order (ARCOM), the domains used by streaming sources. The block happens at the DNS level: your ISP lies when your device asks 'where is this site?'. By using an alternative DNS like Cloudflare 1.1.1.1, you bypass that lie — with no illegal action (changing DNS is 100% allowed).
helpDns.solutionBody: Cloudflare runs a free public DNS, encrypted (DNS-over-HTTPS), fast, with no logs. The 1.1.1.1 app configures everything automatically — no manual settings to touch. You can toggle it on or off in one tap whenever you want.
helpDns.installIosBody: Open the App Store, search for '1.1.1.1: Faster Internet', install it, open it, tap the big button in the middle. Done.
helpDns.installAndroidBody: Open the Play Store, search for '1.1.1.1: Faster Internet', install it, open it, tap the central button. Done.
helpDns.installWindowsBody: Go to one.one.one.one, download the Windows installer, run it. The app configures itself.
helpDns.installMacBody: Go to one.one.one.one, download the macOS app, drag it into Applications. Allow the VPN config when macOS asks.
helpDns.installLinuxBody: No official app. Manually configure 1.1.1.1 and 1.0.0.1 as DNS in your network manager, or on your router (covers every device in your home).
helpDns.otherMirrorsLKS TVHealth: Active mirrors — also listed on LKS TV.online

glossary.seed: Secret 12-word phrase (BIP39 standard) that serves as the unique key to your LKS TV account. No one server-side knows it — if you lose it, the account is permanently lost.
glossary.bip39: BIP39: Bitcoin standard that defines how to generate a 12-word phrase from randomness. This is what LKS TV uses to create your credentials — no email, no password, just that phrase.
glossary.hls: HLS (HTTP Live Streaming): Apple's video streaming format, now used everywhere. The video is cut into small fragments and the player can switch quality on the fly based on your bandwidth.
glossary.embed: Embed: iframe from an external provider (Supervideo, Doodstream, Uqload, etc.) that plays the video with its own interface. Fewer unified controls than a LKS TV HLS player.
glossary.wasm: WebAssembly: binary format executed in the browser, much faster than JavaScript. LKS TV uses it for precise WatchParty synchronization (code written in Rust, compiled to WASM).
glossary.apk: APK (Android Package): installation file for an Android app. You open it once 'unknown sources' is allowed in Android settings, and the app installs just like from the Play Store.
glossary.pwa: PWA (Progressive Web App): website installable on your home screen like a real app — fullscreen, offline cache, notifications. No app store needed, everything goes through the browser.
glossary.arcom: ARCOM: Regulatory Authority for Audiovisual and Digital Communication (France). It's the body that orders French ISPs to block specific streaming domains by court order.

changerLecteur.title: Switch players
changerLecteur.heroSub: Between HLS and embed, how to switch, and why the gear menu is your best friend.
changerLecteur.introBody: On LKS TV, a video can be played by an <1>HLS</1> player (high quality, advanced controls) or an <2>embed</2> player (external provider iframe). The gear button is your entry point to switch between them.
changerLecteur.mockupCaption: Mockup — the gear sits at the bottom-right of the player, next to the time.
changerLecteur.diffHlsBody: Full LKS TV controls, quality menu with bitrate, native subtitles, castable. This is the default whenever an HLS source is available.
changerLecteur.diffEmbedBody: External provider iframe (Supervideo, Voe, Doodstream, Uqload…). The provider's own UI, fewer unified controls. The LKS TV gear button stays your way to switch sources.
changerLecteur.step1: Click the gear icon at the bottom-right of the player.
changerLecteur.step2: Pick a source in the list that appears.
changerLecteur.step3: The player reloads with the chosen source. If a source crashes, auto-fallback moves to the next one with no intervention.

qualiteVideo.title: Video quality
qualiteVideo.heroSub: From 360p to 4K, how to know what you're watching and pick better.
qualiteVideo.introBody: A video's quality depends on two things: player type (HLS or embed) AND the source's original upload.
qualiteVideo.hlsBody1: The gear menu lists every available quality with resolution and estimated bitrate (e.g. 1080p ~ 4500 kbps).
qualiteVideo.hlsBody2: HLS supports adaptive bitrate: the player can switch quality automatically based on your connection.
qualiteVideo.hlsBody3: HLS sources (Nightflix, Nexus HLS, Nexus File, Bravo, Rivestream) can go up to 4K depending on the upload.
qualiteVideo.embedTitle: In an embed player — per provider
qualiteVideo.embedIntro: Each embed provider has its own technical ceiling. These values are generalizations — a given upload may sit below, but never above, the provider's ceiling.
qualiteVideo.embedNote: Quality controls depend on the provider (UI is not unified with LKS TV).
qualiteVideo.checkYourselfBody: To see exactly what's going through the embed iframe (stream URL, real resolution, codec, bitrate), install a 'video stream detector' extension — for example Video DownloadHelper, Stream Detector, or HLS Video Saver. It sniffs media requests in the background and lists the streams with their raw characteristics. This gives you the real info without depending on the provider's UI.
qualiteVideo.tipBody: Want the best quality? Go HLS. Among embeds, Doodstream / Seekstream / Vidplay are the best ceilings (1080p). Avoid Uqload and Sibnet if you're targeting 1080p.

sousTitres.title: Subtitles
sousTitres.heroSub: Turn on, switch, or translate subtitles on both HLS and embed players.
sousTitres.introBody: On LKS TV, subtitles come either from the HLS stream directly (track embedded by the uploader) or from OpenSubtitles for external sources. The HLS gear menu is your single control point.
sousTitres.hlsBody: Open the gear menu, go to the 'Subtitles' section. You get every native track in the stream plus any OpenSubtitles downloads. Click a track to activate, click again to turn off.
sousTitres.externalBody: If the native source doesn't have your language, LKS TV fetches the list of languages available on OpenSubtitles for the movie/episode and shows them in the menu. Pick a language: the track downloads on the fly and becomes active.
sousTitres.translateBody: In the player settings, 'Translate subtitles' option: you can force an automatic translation into the language of your choice. Useful when only the original language track is available.
sousTitres.embedBody: Embeds (Supervideo, Voe, etc.) have their own subtitle UI. No unified LKS TV menu. If the provider doesn't expose a usable track, switch to an HLS player via the gear.

chromecast.title: Chromecast / AirPlay
chromecast.heroSub: Cast a LKS TV video to your TV or compatible device.
chromecast.introBody: LKS TV supports native casting for HLS players. Embed players depend on the provider — often no direct cast.
chromecast.chromecastBody: On desktop Chrome: click the Cast icon in the HLS player controls, pick your Chromecast device. On Android: use the <1>native LKS TV app</1> which integrates Cast.
chromecast.airplayBody: On iOS/macOS Safari: AirPlay icon in the HLS player controls, select Apple TV or compatible HomePod. Safari is required — iOS Chrome doesn't support AirPlay for HLS streams.
chromecast.limitsBody: Cast only works on HLS players (Nightflix, Nexus, Bravo, Rivestream). Embeds (external iframe) are generally not castable. If cast doesn't start, check that your Chromecast/Apple TV is on the same wifi.

compte.title: Create a BIP39 account
compte.heroSub: 12 words, no email, no password. How to create your LKS TV account.
compte.whyBody: LKS TV has no email or password: your identity is a <1>12-word phrase</1> (<2>BIP39</2> standard, the same as Bitcoin). No data collection, no password reset, no phishing emails. You own your account.
compte.step1: Go to LKS TV.chat → Create account.
compte.step2: LKS TV generates 12 random words — shown ONLY ONCE on screen.
compte.step3: Save those 12 words in a password manager, an encrypted file, or on paper. This is your one-and-only key.
compte.step4: Confirm by retyping the words. Your account is created, you're logged in.
compte.warningTitle: Save your seed before closing the screen
compte.warningBody: The 12 words are NEVER shown a second time. If you didn't save them and you lose access to your browser, the account is permanently lost (no recovery possible — see the dedicated tutorial).
compte.oauthBody: You can also create an account via Discord or Google (OAuth). Simpler, but you depend on those platforms. Nothing prevents you from doing both and linking your BIP39 account to an OAuth via the 'Link Discord / Google' tutorial.

recupererCompte.title: Recover a lost account
recupererCompte.heroSub: Spoiler: without the seed, it's impossible. Here's why and how to prevent it.
recupererCompte.warningTitle: Recovery is impossible without your seed
recupererCompte.warningBody: LKS TV stores no version of your <1>seed</1> (not plaintext, not reversibly hashed), and asks for no email at signup. No human at LKS TV can give you access back — there's literally nothing tied to your account server-side that would let anyone re-authenticate you without the seed. That's the fundamental choice of <2>BIP39</2> auth, and it's also what protects you from a database breach.
recupererCompte.whyBody: Any recovery system (email, security question, support-assisted reset) would require storing server-side an element capable of regenerating or replacing your key. That breaks the entire security model: a leak or a social-engineered support agent could cost you the account. Crypto platforms (MetaMask, Ledger, Trust Wallet) work exactly the same — lose the seed, lose access, by design.
recupererCompte.ifLostTitle: If you've already lost your seed
recupererCompte.ifLostBody: If you linked a Discord or Google account to your BIP39 account BEFORE losing the seed, you can reconnect via OAuth on the same browser. Otherwise, the account is permanently lost — create a new one and save the seed this time.
recupererCompte.preventBody: If you still have your seed: copy it into a password manager (Bitwarden, 1Password, Proton Pass) and also print it on paper. Never share with anyone — not even LKS TV support (we will NEVER ask).
recupererCompte.supportBody: Telegram support can help with session issues, login, OAuth, but can do NOTHING about a lost seed. That's not a strict policy, it's a technical impossibility.

lierCompte.title: Link Discord / Google to your BIP39 account
lierCompte.heroSub: Dual auth to recover your account more easily.
lierCompte.introBody: By default, a BIP39 account has only the seed as access method. By linking Discord or Google, you add a second entry door — useful if you lose the seed.
lierCompte.step1: Log in with your seed on LKS TV.
lierCompte.step3: Click 'Link Discord' or 'Link Google', approve the OAuth.
lierCompte.step4: Done. You can now log in with either.
lierCompte.limitsBody: Linking = a SECOND key, not a replacement. If you lose the seed AND you're signed out of Discord/Google on your browser, the account can still be lost (it depends on OAuth session persistence). The seed stays the anchor.
lierCompte.securityBody: If someone hacks your Discord or Google, they also access your LKS TV account. Enable 2FA on Discord/Google before linking. LKS TV stores no OAuth token server-side — just a verified identifier.

watchparty.title: WatchParty
watchparty.heroSub: Watch a movie or episode together, synchronized in real time.
watchparty.introBody: WatchParty opens a shared room: the host starts playback, viewers sync automatically (clock calibration via Rust <1>WASM</1> to stay within a few ms).
watchparty.step1: On a movie/show page, click 'Watch together' (or /watchparty/create).
watchparty.step2: LKS TV generates an invite link — share it with people joining.
watchparty.step3: You're host by default. Start playback, viewers follow automatically.
watchparty.constraintsBody: WatchParty only works with 3 HLS source types: Nexus, Nightflix, and Bravo. They're the only ones where LKS TV controls the pipeline precisely enough to sync. Embeds (Supervideo, Voe, Doodstream, etc.) are NOT supported — sync is impossible without access to the raw stream.
watchparty.incompatBody: For now, Nexus and Bravo do NOT work in WatchParty if you use the LKS TV extension (Chrome/Firefox), the Tampermonkey userscript, or the Android app. The stream goes through a different path that breaks calibration. Use a plain web browser without the extension for WatchParty on those two sources.
watchparty.nightflixNoteBody: Nightflix stays compatible in all cases — it's the source to prefer for WatchParty when you have a choice.
watchparty.syncBody: The status badge at the top of the player shows: 'Calibrating', 'Adjusting', 'Perfect' (< 200ms drift), or 'Unstable' (drift too large, often from one viewer's inconsistent connection). Instability auto-corrects — wait a few seconds.

listesPartagees.title: Shared lists
listesPartagees.heroSub: Create, fill, share a list of movies or shows with your circle.
listesPartagees.introBody: Shared lists let you curate movies/shows and share them via a public link. Collaborative if you invite co-editors.
listesPartagees.step1: Go to Profile → My lists, click 'New list'.
listesPartagees.step2: Give it a name and description. Choose public or private.
listesPartagees.step3: From a movie/show page, click the '+ List' icon to add.
listesPartagees.shareBody: Every list has a stable public URL. Copy the link and send it — people who open it see the list without an account. Subscribed users (logged-in LKS TV) can subscribe to get updates.
listesPartagees.collabBody: In the list settings, you can add co-editors by their LKS TV handle. They can add/remove entries but not delete the list.
listesPartagees.catalogBody: The <1>public catalog</1> aggregates every public list sorted by popularity. Good place to find curated recommendations.

installerPwa.title: Install LKS TV (PWA)
installerPwa.heroSub: Add LKS TV to your home screen for quick access, no app store.
installerPwa.introBody: LKS TV is a <1>PWA</1> (Progressive Web App): you can install it like a real app from your browser. No Play Store or App Store needed. Works on iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, Linux.
installerPwa.iosBody: Open LKS TV.chat in Safari. Tap the share icon (square with an up arrow) → 'Add to Home Screen' → Add. The LKS TV icon appears on your home screen, fullscreen when you open it.
installerPwa.androidBody: Open LKS TV.chat in Chrome. An 'Install app' popup appears at the bottom. Otherwise, menu (⋮) → 'Install app' or 'Add to home screen'.
installerPwa.desktopBody: In the URL bar, look for the install icon (monitor with a down arrow, right of the URL). Click it to install LKS TV as a standalone app.
installerPwa.advantagesBody: Direct access from the home screen, fullscreen UI (no more browser chrome), offline service worker for visited pages, silent auto-update on every launch.

extension.title: Chrome / Firefox extension
extension.heroSub: Unblock certain embed players and speed up stream extraction.
extension.introBody: The LKS TV extension (Chrome and Firefox, manifest v3) runs in the background, pulls stream URLs straight from embed iframes, and lets LKS TV play some sources that would otherwise be blocked. The <1>/extension</1> page centralizes installation and auto-detects whether it's already running.
extension.chromeBody: The extension is NOT on the Chrome Web Store — Google systematically removes extensions that interact with third-party streaming. Two options: (a) easiest — install the Tampermonkey userscript via the <1>/extension</1> page (no developer mode, no startup popup); (b) download the Chrome ZIP from the <2>GitHub repo</2> (extension/Chrome folder) and load it in developer mode (chrome://extensions → enable 'Developer mode' top-right → 'Load unpacked' → select the unzipped folder).
extension.firefoxBody: Same as Chrome: the extension is NOT on addons.mozilla.org (similar Mozilla policy on third-party streaming). Either use the userscript via <1>/extension</1> (easier and persistent), or download the Firefox ZIP from the <2>GitHub repo</2> (extension/Firefox folder) and load it via about:debugging → This Firefox → Load Temporary Add-on — note this is temporary and will be gone on Firefox restart.
extension.permissionsBody: The extension needs access to embed provider pages (Supervideo, Voe, etc.) to read streams. It collects no personal data — everything is open-source and auditable on GitHub.
extension.limitsBody: With the extension enabled, Nexus and Bravo sources do NOT work in WatchParty (see the WatchParty tutorial). If you want to watchparty on those sources, temporarily disable the extension or use Nightflix.

openSource.title: LKS TV is open-source
openSource.heroSub: All the code is public on GitHub. No black box, no lock-in to the official server: fork, audit, self-host, contribute.
openSource.introBody: LKS TV is published open-source on <1>GitHub</1> under the <2>CC BY-NC 4.0</2> license (Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial). In practice: the code is free to read, modify, host, and redistribute — as long as you credit LKS TV and don't use it commercially. No hidden trackers, no silent telemetry: everything the client and the server do is verifiable.
openSource.contentBody: The <1>LKS TVOpenSource</1> monorepo contains everything: the React/Vite frontend (src/ folder), the Node.js and Python APIs (API/ folder — Express for the main one, aiohttp for the embed proxies, Flask for bypass403, Socket.IO for WatchParty), the Chrome MV3 / Firefox MV2 / Tampermonkey userscript extensions (extension/ and userscript/ folders), the Android WebView app (app/ folder), the WatchParty sync engine written in Rust compiled to WebAssembly (wasm/watchparty-sync/ folder), the Cloudflare Workers for CORS relays (cloudflareproxy/ folder), and the Discord Rich Presence plugin (PreMid/ folder).
openSource.whyBody: Transparency first: you can verify what your browser runs and what the server stores. Your BIP39 account (12-word seed) is handled client-side — the server never sees your seed, and you can go read the code to prove it. Resilience next: if the main domain goes down or if LKS TV disappears, anyone can clone, build, and host. The project doesn't depend on any proprietary service to run. Trust last: many streaming platforms are closed and opaque — here everything is auditable, and every commit is public.
openSource.selfhostBody: You can clone the <1>repo</1> and run LKS TV locally or on your own server. The README and CLAUDE.md at the root document the stack, env vars (~100 for the main API), commands (npm run dev for the frontend on :3000, node server.js for the main API on :25565, python server.py for the embed proxies on :25569, etc.) and the service architecture. You'll need MySQL, Redis, and TMDB / Turnstile / Discord&Google OAuth keys depending on what you enable. The instance you spin up is fully independent of LKS TV.chat — your users, your database, your control.
openSource.contribBody: PRs are welcome on the <1>GitHub repo</1>. Bugs, translations (especially English — French is the primary language, many EN strings still need polish), new sources, UX improvements, new tutorials to add to the hub: all helpful. For small fixes, PR directly. For big architecture changes, open an issue first so we can talk it through before you spend three days on it.
openSource.limitsTitle: What the license does NOT allow
openSource.limitsBody: The <1>CC BY-NC 4.0</1> license forbids commercial use. In practice: no reselling access, no monetization via aggressive ads, no disguised clone behind a paywall. Free self-hosting for you and your friends: yes. Paid self-hosting to make money: no. Attribution ("based on LKS TV, link to the repo") is also mandatory — silent forks are forbidden.

appMobile.title: Android mobile app
appMobile.heroSub: LKS TV as an Android app, with built-in cast, embedded extension and 1.1.1.1 DNS toggle.
appMobile.introBody: The Android LKS TV app is a WebView that loads LKS TV with a few native integrations: Chromecast, embedded LKS TV extension (no Tampermonkey needed), 1.1.1.1 DNS toggle (local VPN that only redirects DNS queries to bypass ISP blocking), and automatic update popup. Everything is centralized on the <1>/app</1> page.
appMobile.downloadBody: The <1>APK</1> is distributed directly by LKS TV (not on the Play Store — Google doesn't accept streaming apps that aggregate third-party sources). Direct download on the <2>/app</2> page, which fetches LKS TV-android.apk from the official GitHub repo.
appMobile.step1: Download the APK file from the /app page.
appMobile.step2: In Android Settings → Security → Allow unknown sources for your browser (one-time step per browser).
appMobile.step3: Open the downloaded APK, tap 'Install'. The LKS TV icon appears in the drawer.
appMobile.updatesBody: On every launch, the app checks the online version. A 'New version available' popup appears when an update is ready. Tap to download, install over the existing app. No reconfig needed.
appMobile.iosBody: No iOS app for the same reasons: Apple doesn't allow third-party streaming apps. Workaround: install LKS TV as a PWA from Safari (see the 'Install PWA' tutorial).

caMarchePlus.title: Nothing works anymore
caMarchePlus.heroSub: The 4 most common causes when LKS TV won't load, in the order to try.
caMarchePlus.intro: If the site won't load or no player starts, try these causes in order. It's cause number 1 eight times out of ten.
caMarchePlus.cause1Title: My ISP is blocking LKS TV
caMarchePlus.cause1Body: French ISPs (Orange, SFR, Free, Bouygues) regularly block our domains and streaming sources by court order (<1>ARCOM</1>). Most of the time, this is the culprit.
caMarchePlus.cause2Body: Some adblockers kill extraction scripts or block source domains. Try whitelisting LKS TV.chat and disable temporarily to test. We recommend Brave + uBlock Origin, the most stable combo on LKS TV.
caMarchePlus.cause3Body: The main domain can be down briefly. The live mirror list is published both on our own domain LKS TV.online and on rentry.co/LKS TV.
caMarchePlus.cause4Body: If nothing works despite the above, it's likely a LKS TV bug or a source down on the provider side. Report it on Telegram.

premid.title: PreMiD extension (Discord Rich Presence)
premid.heroSub: Show what you're watching on LKS TV directly in your Discord presence.
premid.introBody: <1>PreMiD</1> is a browser extension that reads what you're doing on certain websites and forwards it to your Discord desktop client, which displays it as your current activity. LKS TV has its own official integration: while watching a movie or show, your Discord friends see the title, the current episode, and even the remaining time.
premid.installBody: PreMiD runs as two pieces: a browser extension + the Discord desktop app running in parallel. Install <1>PreMiD for Chrome</1> or <2>PreMiD for Firefox</2>. Discord must be the desktop app (Discord web doesn't support Rich Presence).
premid.step1: Install the PreMiD extension in your browser.
premid.step2: Open the extension settings → Presences tab → search for « LKS TV ».
premid.step3: Click « Add presence » on LKS TV. That's it.
premid.step4: Open LKS TV in a tab, start a movie. The activity shows up in your Discord profile a few seconds later.
premid.LKS TVPresenceBody: The LKS TV presence is published and maintained at <1>premid.app/fr/library/LKS TV</1>. It shows the movie/show title, the episode (for shows), the elapsed / remaining time, and a « Watch » button that brings your friends back to the page. Open-source, the code lives in the `PreMid/` folder of the LKS TV repo.
premid.limitsBody: Rich Presence only works if the Discord desktop app is running (not web, not mobile). The PreMiD extension must be active on the LKS TV tab. In private browsing, the extension is disabled by default — you have to explicitly allow it in the browser settings.

profils.title: Profiles & parental controls
profils.heroSub: Multiple people on the same account, each with their own avatar, lists and age restrictions.
profils.introBody: A LKS TV account can have several profiles. Each profile has its own avatar, watch history, lists and age restriction. Useful if you share the account with a sibling, your parents, or if you just want to separate your « movies » profile from your « shows » profile. Profiles are managed from the <1>Manage profiles</1> page.
profils.step1: Go to « Manage profiles » from the user menu (top-right).
profils.step3: Pick a name, an avatar, and an age restriction if needed.
profils.step4: Confirm — the profile shows up on the selection screen next time you launch.
profils.ageTableIntro: The age restriction filters movies and shows against the TMDB rating. A 16+ movie won't appear in results for a profile set to 12+.
profils.age18Meaning: No age filtering, same as « None » but shown as an adult profile.
profils.ageTableNote: Restrictions apply to lists, search and detail pages. They don't encrypt anything — it's a display filter, not a cryptographic lock.
profils.kidsBody: For a kid profile, combine a 7+ or 12+ restriction with a dedicated avatar. The UI stays the same as for an adult profile (no separate « kids mode » yet), but the visible catalog is filtered. Tip: use the kid's name so the profile picker at startup is obvious.
profils.switchBody: You can switch profiles without logging out: click your avatar top-right → « Switch profile » → pick another one. History and lists follow the active profile, not the account. The <1>Profile selection</1> page also shows up on every new login.
profils.limitsBody: No per-profile PIN / password yet: anyone with account access can switch to any profile. If you really need to block access to a profile (e.g. a curious child), create a separate account for them. Age restrictions protect against accidental display, not against a determined user.

vip.title: Become VIP
vip.heroSub: Support pack payable in crypto or by card, unlocks LKS TV's advanced features.
vip.introBody: Becoming VIP supports the project (servers, metadata APIs, paid sources, dev) and unlocks a bundle of exclusive features in return. Purchase on the <1>/vip/don</1> page, active immediately after payment is confirmed.
vip.perksBody: Ad-free players (no more pre-roll iframe ads), new <1>WatchParty</1>-compatible Nexus and Bravo players, priority requests to the team, VIP badge in comments, full access to France TV (live + replay), premium Live TV sources (WiFlix, SosPlay/Bolaloca, international sports streams), and most importantly the built-in Debrid service to generate direct 1fichier links with no wait and no captcha.
vip.packsIntro: All packs unlock the same features — the only difference is duration. One-time payment, not a recurring subscription.
vip.paymentBody: Bitcoin (BTC) and Litecoin (LTC) available on every pack (crypto = max privacy, no data collection). PayGate.to (card, Apple Pay, Google Pay) available starting at €7 (the €5 pack is too low to cover card processing fees). Payblis (card, PayPal, Revolut, SEPA, Open Banking) may be enabled depending on site configuration. Card options require a valid email for the receipt — that email is not tied to your LKS TV account, it's just for the invoice.
vip.step2: Pick a pack (amount + duration), then the payment method (BTC, LTC, or card).
vip.step3: For crypto: scan the QR or copy the address + exact amount, send from your wallet. For card: you're redirected to PayGate/Payblis which handles checkout.
vip.step4: Wait for confirmation (1-3 blockchain confirmations for crypto, instant for card). Your VIP status shows up on your profile as soon as it's validated — refresh if needed.
vip.giftBody: From <1>/vip/don</1>, you can switch to 'Gift' mode: instead of activating VIP on your account, LKS TV generates a unique link for you to send to the recipient. They log in with that link, the token is consumed, and their account becomes VIP. Handy for gifting to a friend without knowing their LKS TV handle.
vip.invoicesBody: All your VIP transactions show up on <1>/vip/invoices</1> — amount, date, method, status (pending / confirmed / expired), and a link to the public detail (/vip/invoice/:publicId) if you want to share proof of payment (e.g. for support).
vip.limitsBody: No refunds possible: crypto or card, once the payment is validated it's final. We always recommend starting with the €5 pack for your first purchase — it's a low-cost test to confirm VIP activates properly on your account before committing to a longer pack. If you'd rather test before paying, reach out to Telegram support: we can enable a free ~3-day trial so you can see what VIP looks like on your account before deciding.

debrid.title: Debrid 1fichier links
debrid.heroSub: VIP feature: turn a 1fichier link into an instant direct download, no wait, no captcha.
debrid.introBody: The <1>LKS TV debrider</1> takes a 1fichier link (the kind that normally forces you to wait 30 seconds + solve a captcha + download at 200 KB/s without a premium account) and returns a full-speed direct download link. LKS TV doesn't resell premium access — we relay your request to multiple debrider services (Real-Debrid, BestDebrid, Deepbrid) and return the best result.
debrid.vipBody: The debrider is a <1>VIP</1>-exclusive feature. No VIP = greyed-out button + 'Become VIP' popup. The Real-Debrid / BestDebrid / Deepbrid API keys LKS TV uses are paid server-side, and debriding consumes quota — that's why it's limited to accounts that support the project.
debrid.supportedBody: Only 1fichier is currently supported on LKS TV (it's the most common in the download-link ecosystem). The backend providers can technically handle Uptobox, Rapidgator, Turbobit, Nitroflare, Mega, etc., but the LKS TV UI currently only exposes 1fichier. Hosts like Dropgalaxy, Fileal, Filedot, Filespace, Gigapeta, Katfile, Worldbytez are NOT supported (quotas exhausted or unreliable).
debrid.providersBody: Three providers are available in the dropdown: Real-Debrid (link generated LKS TV proxy-side — the most stable), BestDebrid (link generated browser-side with your key fetched from the LKS TV API — fastest), Deepbrid (alternate fallback). If one crashes (exhausted quota, expired key), try the others: they often use different account pools.
debrid.step1: Go to /debrid from the menu or from a 'Debrid' button on the download page.
debrid.step2: Paste your 1fichier link (e.g. https://1fichier.com/?abc123) into the field.
debrid.step3: Pick a provider (Real-Debrid by default), then click 'Debrid'.
debrid.step4: The direct link shows up in seconds — click 'Download the file' to start the full-speed download, or copy the link to pass it to your download manager (JDownloader, etc.).
debrid.historyBody: The last 50 debrided links are stored locally (localStorage) — no server-side history, no tracking. You can re-debrid an old link (the direct link expires after a few hours provider-side; re-debriding regenerates a fresh one), remove it from history, or clear everything at once.
debrid.limitsBody: Direct links expire provider-side (typically 3-24h). Debriding consumes LKS TV-side quota — in case of abusive usage, a rate-limit may kick in. The provider can also temporarily crash if its upstream premium accounts run out of quota. If you hit a repeated error, wait 10 minutes and retry with another provider.

telechargement.title: Download a movie or episode
telechargement.heroSub: Grab the download links from the movie or episode detail page, with or without debrid.
telechargement.introBody: On a movie or show page, the 'Download' button takes you to a dedicated page (/download/movie/:id or /download/tv/:id) that aggregates links from multiple hosting providers (1fichier, Sendcm, DarkiBox, etc.) with quality, language, subtitles and size. It's complementary to streaming: useful for keeping a local copy, watching offline on a flight, or bypassing a stream issue.
telechargement.step1: Go to a movie or show detail page, click the 'Download' button (down-arrow icon).
telechargement.step2: For a series: pick the season then the episode. For a movie: the page shows the available links directly.
telechargement.step3: Pick a link based on quality (480p, 720p, 1080p, 4K), language (VF, VOSTFR, Multi) and host.
telechargement.step4: Click the link — you're redirected to the provider's download page. Follow its instructions (free wait, or premium/debrid access if you have VIP).
telechargement.hostsBody: The main providers are 1fichier (very common, supports LKS TV debriding), Sendcm, DarkiBox, and occasionally others depending on the source. Availability depends on the original uploader — not every title is on every host.
telechargement.debridTitle: Speed it up with VIP debrider
telechargement.debridBody: For 1fichier links, if you're <2>VIP</2>, use the built-in 'Debrid' button: it turns the link into a full-speed direct download, with no 30-second wait and no captcha. See the <1>debrider tutorial</1> for details. For other hosts (Sendcm, DarkiBox), there's no LKS TV debriding — you go through the free wait or use an external premium account.
telechargement.seriesBody: For series, the page shows available seasons and episodes with their links. Each episode can have multiple qualities/languages. Recent seasons may be missing episodes — that just means the uploader hasn't published the release on tracked hosts yet.
telechargement.limitsBody: Links can expire (file removed host-side, anti-piracy policy change). Some providers geoblock — a VPN solves that. Non-VIP users may have to watch an ad before decoded links are revealed.

miroirs.title: The site won't open — which mirror should I use?
miroirs.heroSub: How LKS TV redirects you to an alive domain when the main one is blocked, and where to find the official list.
miroirs.introBody: French ISPs (Orange, SFR, Free, Bouygues) regularly block LKS TV domains on <1>ARCOM</1> orders. To keep you connected through blocks, LKS TV installs a service worker in your browser that detects network failure and automatically redirects you to a still-alive mirror — no manual URL lookup needed.
miroirs.howBody: When you visit LKS TV.chat, a service worker (a silent piece of code) installs in your browser. If LKS TV.chat later becomes unreachable (3-second timeout or DNS error), the service worker fetches the alive-mirror list from an external non-blocked source (rentry.co/LKS TV, a pastebin service hosted outside France) and redirects you to the first available mirror. Transparent, instant, zero config.
miroirs.officialListTitle: Where to see the official list
miroirs.officialListBody: Two places publish the canonical alive-mirror list: <1>LKS TV.online</1> (domain status with real-time up/down indicator) and <2>rentry.co/LKS TV</2> (raw list, edited by the LKS TV team, always current). If you doubt a URL claiming to be LKS TV, check it against one of these — anything NOT on the list is a fake (sacrificial domains designed to fool anti-piracy bots, or phishing attempts).
miroirs.newDeviceTitle: New phone / new browser — the redirect doesn't kick in
miroirs.newDeviceBody: The service worker can only redirect you if it's been installed at least once before the block. On a brand-new device that has never visited LKS TV.chat, if the domain is already ISP-blocked, you'll hit an ISP error page with no redirect. Solution: open LKS TV.online or rentry.co/LKS TV from any browser (those pages are hosted outside the ISP block), grab an alive mirror, and visit it. Or join <1>Telegram @LKS TV_site</1> where the list is always up to date.
miroirs.reliabilityBody: A domain can go down overnight (new ARCOM order) or stay stable for months. The team rotates multiple domains in parallel — when one goes down, another takes over automatically. The rentry.co list is updated within 24h of any change.
miroirs.limitsBody: The service worker caches its internal config for 24h — so if you add a brand-new mirror on rentry, existing clients see it within 24h max. If your ISP ALSO blocks rentry.co or LKS TV.online (rare but possible), the fallback breaks entirely: route through Telegram or use an alternate DNS like 1.1.1.1 (see the DNS tutorial). The service worker doesn't always work in private browsing — use a regular tab.

liveTv.title: Live TV — channels and live sports
liveTv.heroSub: Watch TF1, M6, BFM, live football matches and international sports streams from LKS TV.
liveTv.introBody: The <1>Live TV</1> page aggregates live television channels (generalist, news, cinema, sport, kids) and international sports streams (football, basketball, rugby, tennis, combat, motorsport). Some of it is free to all, the rest is VIP-gated depending on the source.
liveTv.sourcesBody: Linkzy (free, generalist French channels + sport + cinema), SosPlay/Bolaloca (free mix of international channels), LiveTV (huge catalog of live sport matches — football, hockey, basketball, tennis, volleyball, handball, rugby, combat, motorsport, winter sports, athletics), WiFlixFR (premium French channels — TF1, M6, BFM, Canal+, etc. with replay), and France TV (all France Télévisions channels + replay). The last two require a <1>VIP account</1>; the others are open to everyone.
liveTv.categoriesIntro: Channels are grouped by category — you can filter in the catalog to find what you're looking for.
liveTv.catGeneralisteExample: TF1, M6, France 2, France 3, Linkzy generalist
liveTv.catCinemaExample: Cine+, Linkzy cinema channels, WiFlix cinema
liveTv.catInfoExample: BFMTV, CNews, LCI, France Info, Euronews
liveTv.catSportExample: Football (Ligue 1, Champions League), basketball NBA/Euroleague, rugby, tennis, F1, MotoGP, boxing
liveTv.catEnfantsExample: Gulli, Tiji, Disney Channel, Cartoon Network, WiFlix kids
liveTv.catMusiqueExample: MTV, M6 Music, NRJ Hits, Trace
liveTv.catDocuExample: RMC Découverte, Ushuaïa TV, Planète+, WiFlix documentary channels
liveTv.matchesBody: The LiveTV source publishes the day's matches with kickoff time, competition and a link to the live stream. A 'LIVE' badge appears when the match is in progress. Very useful for Ligue 1 or Champions League matches broadcast on paid channels (Canal+, DAZN, beIN). Quality and stability depend on the upstream provider — this is unofficial streaming, so sometimes unstable.
liveTv.favoritesBody: You can mark any channel or source as a favorite (star icon). The favorites list is stored locally in your browser (localStorage) — it doesn't sync across devices for now. Handy to quickly get back to your regular channels without re-searching.
liveTv.extensionBody: A few Live TV sources (WiFlix and some LiveTV sport streams) require the <1>LKS TV browser extension</1> to extract the stream — otherwise the page shows an 'Extension required' message. The Android app already embeds the extension by default, nothing to install in that case.
liveTv.limitsBody: Variable stability: live TV streams are relayed from third-party sources that can drop without notice. Quality ranges from 480p to 1080p depending on source. Some French channels (particularly sport) geoblock to France — no issue if you're in France. Outside, use a VPN or a DNS like <1>1.1.1.1</1>. Kids channels have no dedicated parental control on Live TV (use a separate adult/kids LKS TV profile). Live matches can lag 10-60 seconds behind the official broadcast — that's the cost of rebroadcasting.

extraction.title: Control the extractors
extraction.heroSub: Disable individual m3u8 hosters or Live TV sources from Settings.
extraction.introBody: LKS TV uses 'extractors' to fetch the video stream (m3u8) from each hoster — Voe, Uqload, Sibnet, Vidmoly, Vidzy, Fsvid, Doodstream, SeekStreaming — and from Live TV sources (Vavoo, Wiflix, SOSPLAY, LiveTV876). You can disable each one individually when an extractor breaks, slows down, or if you'd rather play that hoster as a plain iframe.
extraction.whereBody: Go to Settings → Extractors. Two distinct cards: m3u8 Extractors (movies & series) and Live TV Sources. Each card has a master toggle to enable/disable everything in one click, plus one toggle per service.
extraction.step1: Disable an extractor via its toggle. The site filters that extractor instantly — the next source attempt will skip it.
extraction.step2: The Chrome / Firefox extension and the userscript receive the setting automatically (sync via postMessage). No need to configure each side separately.
extraction.step3: The category-level master toggle lets you mass-switch — handy to test if an issue comes from the m3u8 category or the Live TV category.
extraction.cacheBody: If the extension is installed, a counter shows the number of session extractions per hoster ('12 extractions') along with the cached entry count. The 'Clear cache' button purges the streams cached by the extension — useful if a stream broke and you want to force a fresh extraction.
extraction.noExtensionBody: Toggles still work (they write to localStorage), but stats and cache are hidden. When you install the extension later, your preferences are automatically pushed back — nothing to reconfigure.
extraction.limitsBody: Disabling an extractor doesn't break anything as long as other sources are available: the player tries the next one. If you disable everything in a category and no alternative source works, the player shows 'no player available'. Extractions in flight at the moment of the toggle are not cancelled — the setting applies to the next one.

prioriteSources.title: Source priority
prioriteSources.heroSub: Reorder sources and hosters, pin your favorites, create custom hosters with regex.
prioriteSources.introBody: When you launch a movie or episode, LKS TV tries multiple sources in parallel (FStream, Lynx, Nightflix, Omega, Multi, Frembed, Viper, Vox, Nexus, Bravo…) and auto-selects the first available one. By default the order is fixed. This section lets you define YOUR order — to favor sources you find most reliable or fastest at home.
prioriteSources.whereBody: Go to Settings → Priority. Two tabs: Movies & Series on one side, Anime on the other (anime is sorted by language: VF, VOSTFR, VJ, VA…). Each tab has a Sources list (top-level) and a Hoster Order list (Voe, Uqload, etc.).
prioriteSources.step1: Drag-and-drop: grab the <1/> handle on the left of a source to move it up or down. Changes are saved instantly.
prioriteSources.step2: On/off toggle: completely disable a top-level source (FStream, Lynx, etc.) — it will never be tried. Individual hosters (Voe, Uqload…) are managed from Settings → Extractors, not here.
prioriteSources.step3: Pin <2/>: pin a source as #1. It moves to the top, and a snapshot of the pre-pin order is kept. Click again = unpin and restore order. Manual drag = pin auto-cleared.
prioriteSources.step4: Per-source override: click the <3/> icon on a source to open a modal that lets you define a hoster order specific to THAT source (e.g. 'in Lynx I prefer Uqload > Voe', but everywhere else keep the global order).
prioriteSources.pinBody: The pin <1/> doesn't only live in Settings: you'll find it directly in the player (on source selection buttons) and in the HLS player's Servers menu (on each hoster). Pinning from the player → the Settings page reflects immediately, and vice versa. Only one active pin per category (sources / hosters).
prioriteSources.overrideBody: Clicking <1/> opens a modal with a 'Use global order' switch. Disable it to define a custom hoster order for THIS context only. Concrete example: Uqload is faster than Voe on Lynx at home, but Voe is still better everywhere else → make a Lynx override without touching the global order.
prioriteSources.advancedBody: Collapsible section at the bottom of the panel. Two capabilities: add domain patterns to built-in hosters (e.g. a brand-new Voe mirror on a new domain → add the regex and LKS TV detects it as Voe); create custom hosters with their name and regex patterns. A custom hoster without a backend extractor plays as an iframe (no extracted m3u8, no unified quality menu — [iframe] badge shown in the list). Limit: 20 patterns max per hoster, live regex validation.
prioriteSources.resetBody: Each sub-section has its own <1/> icon: Sources, Hoster Order, Overrides, Custom Hosters. The 'Reset all' button at the top of the panel resets everything after confirmation. Each reset triggers a Sonner toast with a 5s Undo button that exactly restores the pre-reset state — in case of a misclick.
prioriteSources.syncBody: The order is automatically pushed to the Chrome / Firefox extension and the userscript via postMessage (SET_SOURCE_PRIORITY handler). Multi-tab: a change in one tab is reflected in the others via the 'storage' event. The LKS TV backend also syncs your preferences if you're logged in (generic /api/sync mechanism, nothing to configure).
prioriteSources.limitsBody: Live TV is not affected — channels have their own independent fallback logic. No priority per individual movie or episode: only one global order per category (Movies & Series / Anime). Custom hosters without a backend extractor only play as iframes (no LKS TV quality controls). An old extension without the SET_SOURCE_PRIORITY handler silently ignores the setting (backward compat OK).

dernierLecteur.title: Remember the last player
dernierLecteur.heroSub: Automatically reuse the player you just selected on the next movie or episode.
dernierLecteur.introBody: This option keeps track of the last player (Darkino, FStream, Lynx, Omega, Bravo, Viper, Vox, Frembed, Nexus, MP4, LKS TV Player…) that you manually picked from the source menu. When enabled, the next movie or episode tries to reuse that same player as long as it's available for the content — otherwise LKS TV falls back to your usual source priority order.
dernierLecteur.howBody: Go to Settings → Source priority. The toggle <1/> labelled "Remember last chosen player" sits right above the priority panel. Flip it on and you're done: your selection is saved every time you change the player from the Servers menu. Off by default (opt-in).
dernierLecteur.priorityBody: When the option is enabled and a last player is remembered, it wins over the user-defined source order in the priority panel <1/>. So even if Darkino sits at the top of your list, if you just watched an episode with FStream and FStream is available on the next one, FStream is reused.
dernierLecteur.fallbackBody: If the remembered player isn't available for the new content (e.g. you were on Darkino but the movie has no Darkino version), LKS TV automatically falls back to your standard priority order — as if the option wasn't enabled for this specific content.
dernierLecteur.limitsBody: Tracking only triggers when you manually pick a player from the Servers menu. Automatic fallbacks on error are NOT remembered — so LKS TV doesn't memorise a backup player that doesn't reflect your intent. The memory is global (not per movie): only one player is kept at a time, the most recent one. No hoster granularity (Voe / Uqload…): only the top-level player is remembered.

apparence.title: Customize the appearance
apparence.heroSub: Background style and color, smooth scroll, scroll memory, effects and other visual settings.
apparence.introBody: Settings → Appearance gathers all visual customization options for LKS TV. Everything is stored locally in your browser (localStorage) — nothing is sent to the server, so each device can have its own setup.
apparence.bgStyleBody: Three modes: Combined (static background + light animations, default), Static (just the squares pattern, most performant), Animated (full animation, more demanding). If you feel micro-stutters on a modest device, switch to Static — it frees up significant GPU load.
apparence.bgColorBody: You can customize the background accent color via a dedicated color picker, and adjust the squares size with a slider. The smaller the squares, the finer and denser the pattern. Useful to fit your screen (a fine pattern on a large screen, or chunkier squares on mobile).
apparence.smoothScrollBody: Enables inertia on mouse scroll (powered by Lenis). Three intensities: Standard (fast response, little glide), Fluid (balanced), Ultra fluid (heavy inertia, 'cinematic' feel). If you don't enjoy the gliding feel, fully disable — LKS TV falls back to native scroll. Note: smooth scroll is disabled by default on the Settings page itself to avoid interfering with side navigation.
apparence.scrollPositionBody: Two distinct toggles. 'Preserve between pages' keeps your scroll position when navigating from one page to another (instead of jumping to top on each transition). 'Preserve on back' keeps the scroll when you go back (history). Useful to resume a catalog browsing session without losing your place.
apparence.soundBody: Enables micro UI sounds (click, hover, transitions). Disabled by default. No aggressive notification sounds — only discreet feedback.
apparence.commentsBody: Hides the comments section on movie / series / anime pages. Useful to avoid spoilers or if you find it weighs the page down.
apparence.heroBody: Hides the large cinematic carousel at the top of the home page (the auto-scrolling 'hero' that cycles through movies / series). Recommended if you're on a TV (Android TV, slow browser), a low-power device, or if you find the banner distracting — it eases GPU load and prevents freezes during home load. You then drop straight into the Trending, Top, Continue rows on open.
apparence.snowBody: Enables a falling snowflake effect in the background. More of a seasonal toggle (winter), turn on / off as you wish. Light on perf, but visually strong.
apparence.screensaverBody: Displays a cinematic screensaver after a period of inactivity (mouse / keyboard). Two settings: the delay (30 s, 1, 2, 5 or 10 min) and the style — Cinematic carousel (full-screen movie / series backdrops cycling), or Favorite mosaic (animated grid of your favorited movies and series). The slightest mouse motion or key press dismisses it instantly. Useful in demo mode or when leaving LKS TV open on a TV.
apparence.introAnimationBody: Enables a small 'periodic table' style animation (Breaking Bad nod) when the site loads. If you find it tedious on every hard reload, or want to shave off a few hundred ms at startup, turn it off. The animation doesn't replay during in-app navigation — only on initial entry into LKS TV.
apparence.sidebarBody: On the Settings page, a small vertical indicator slides along the sidebar to point at the active section as you scroll. If you find it distracting, the drag-axis is locked (vertical only) and the indicator does a clean fade-out when you click to navigate elsewhere.
apparence.limitsBody: All these settings are local (your browser's localStorage): they are not synced across devices. If you use LKS TV on desktop + mobile, configure each device separately. If you link an account (BIP39 or OAuth), a future generic sync may include these preferences — for now they remain local by design.
